title: Sleeping Beauty
content:
Long ago, a king and queen had a baby daughter and were filled with joy. They held a great celebration and invited all the good fairies of the kingdom. One by one, the fairies gave the baby gifts of beauty, grace, and kindness. But in their happiness, they had forgotten to invite one old fairy who lived alone in a dark tower.

This forgotten fairy arrived, trembling with anger. She leaned over the baby. "Here is my gift," she said coldly. "On her sixteenth birthday, the princess will prick her finger on a spindle and fall down dead!" Then she vanished. The king and queen wept with terror.

But one young fairy who had not yet given her gift stepped forward bravely. "I cannot undo the curse," she said, "but I can soften it. The princess will not die. Instead, she will fall into a deep sleep for one hundred years, until true love's kiss wakes her." The king ordered every spindle in the kingdom to be burned.

The princess grew up and was everything the fairies had promised: beautiful, gracious, and kind. On the morning of her sixteenth birthday, she explored a part of the castle she had never visited. She climbed a narrow stair and found a hidden tower room.

Inside sat an old woman at a spinning wheel. The princess had never seen a spindle before. She reached out, curious. The moment it pricked her finger, she fell into a deep, deep sleep. In that very instant, everyone in the castle fell asleep too.

Thorny vines and tall trees grew up around the castle until it was completely swallowed by the forest. A hundred years passed. Then a young prince came riding through the forest and spotted tall towers above the treetops. He cut through the vines and found the silent castle.

He walked through the sleeping halls until he found the princess in the tower, sleeping peacefully. Moved by her peaceful face, he leaned down and kissed her gently. Her eyes opened and she smiled. All around the castle, everyone woke up. The long sleep was over and happiness had returned to every corner of the kingdom.

author: Charles Perrault
